ocr.text: The data table presented offers a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between residency type and various neurological
conditions, specifically Parkinson's Disease (PD) and Dementia with Lewy Bodies (DLB). The examination of coefficients and
their corresponding p values reveals critical insights into the statistical significance of these associations. Notably, the
coefficient for residency type is 5.4, with a p value of 0.09, suggesting a potential but not statistically conclusive correlation
between this variable and neurological outcomes. Conversely, the coefficient of 3.1, paired with a p value of 0.40, indicates a
weaker association within the demographic segment of the study. The table also delineates the maximum number of unknown
parameters (I) and the obtainable information (II), further elucidating the complexity of the data. The difference between these
two metrics, represented as the maximum information deficit (III), provides a clearer understanding of the gaps in the current
knowledge base. This analysis serves to inform future research directions and highlight the need for more robust data collection
methodologies within this field.
